summary: Forensic comparison of original English shotgun script vs Spanish rewritten version. Identifies three layers of content mirroring that triggered content moderation flags: (1) rhetorical comparisons copied verbatim (DP-12 vs M4 collapsed benchmark), (2) argument structure mirrored per product (Saiga sanctions narrative arc, VR80 competition-vs-combat pivot, TS12 training-cost framing), (3) emotional arcs replicated (M4 chapter is near carbon-copy). Root cause traced to extract blueprint prompt's 'include EVERYTHING' rule, which fails to distinguish between verifiable specs and author's creative rhetoric. Proposes 3-layer fix: extract prompt separates fact vs rhetoric, outline prompt forces original argument angles, writer prompt strengthens anti-mirroring rule.
